destination: Damīra, Jūjar, Sammanūd, Shubrā Damsīs, Sunbāṭ
description: Recto: Circular letter in the name of Moshe Maimonides to the congregations in Egypt (ca. 1168–1204 CE). The recto preserves only an eloquent introduction. Verso gives the name of Moshe Maimonides and preserves the address to the notables of Damīra, Jujar, Sammanūd, Damsīs, and Sunbāṭ. Also on verso is a Judaeo-Arabic note at 180 degrees about the story of Jacob and Leah. (Information in part from Goitein's note card.) BL OR 5533.1, T-S 12.238, and T-S 16.9 are all versions of the same letter.
description_he: מכתב חוזר בשם משה מימוניד לקהילות מצרים, כ-1168-1204, הפנייה לנכבדי דמירה, ג'וג'ר, סמנוד, דמסיס וסונבאט
